content:
RUST | PROGRAMMER | PROGRAMMING LANGUAGE | SOFTWARE ENGINEERING
Don’t Overlook it. These Features will Make Your Code Cleaner, Safer, and More Expressive.

11 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Not flashy, but they make our code cleaner, safer, and more expressive.
Not every useful Rust feature gets the attention it deserves. Most Rust developers naturally focus on ownership, borrowing, lifetimes, and concurrency when learning the language, and that’s a good thing. These concepts form the foundation of Rust.
However, Rust also offers many lesser-known features and idiomatic patterns that can significantly improve the way we write code. They can reduce boilerplate, make APIs more ergonomic, express intent more clearly, and help us leverage Rust’s type system more effectively. While each feature may seem small on its own, together they make our code cleaner, safer, more expressive, and easier to maintain.
